Notice Of Motion To Withdraw Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted
Off Calendar Dec-01-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
ALEXANDER M. MERCHANT, ESQ., ELKE & MERCHANT LLP (415) 294-4111 alex@elkemerchant.com
Attorneys for Plaintiff Patricia Lansche.
JOSEPH K. BRAVO, ESQ., BRAVO LAW OFFICES (415) 512-6700 joebravo@bravolaw.com Attorney for Defendant William Dean Melovich et al

All parties stipulate to Judge Pro tem James Fleming hearing today's motion. The court and parties present authorize the clerk to sign the Judge Pro Tem stipulaiton on their behalf.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as modified:

DEFENDANT WILLIAM MELOVICH'S Motion To Withdraw Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted

Defendants' previous attorney moved to be relieved as counsel but, due to one or more mailing errors on the part of previous counsel, Defendants were unaware of the withdrawal proceedings and the order granting motion to be relieved dated March 1, 2023. Thereafter, Defendants received various mailings from Plaintiff's counsel but did not open the envelopes on the mistaken but reasonable belief of Defendant Joseph Melovich that he was represented by counsel and the infirmity of Defendant Annabel Melovich. Defendant Joseph Melovich did not learn of the withdrawal until he received a call from the Court's Settlement Conference Officer on August 28, 2023. Defendants diligently retained new counsel on September 18, 2023, and filed motions to continue the trial, reopen discovery, and withdraw requests for admissions deemed admitted. In addition, Defendants promptly served responses to the outstanding requests for admissions and interrogatories.

It appears to the satisfaction of the Court that due to inadvertence or excusable neglect, largely on the part of previous counsel, Defendants were unaware of the requests for admission, motion to deem matters admitted, and order thereon. (CCP 473; Melovich and Bravo Declarations.) The Court has a policy to hear cases on the merits and slight evidence is necessary to obtain relief from a default. Miller v. City of Hermosa Beach, 13 Cal.App.4th 1118, 1136 (1993). Numerous courts have granted relief in similar circumstances. Pelegrinelli v. McCloud River Lumber Co. 1 Cal App. 593 (1905) (vacating default where president of corporation put summons and complaint in his drawer and did not forward them to the insurer.)

Under the circumstances and in the interests of justice, the Motion to Withdraw Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted is granted. Pursuant to CCP 473(c)(1)(C), Defendants shall pay $1,000 in fees and costs to Plaintiff by December 31, 2023, as reasonable expenses for obtaining the order that requests for admissions be deemed admitted. Rogalski v. Nabers Cadillac, 11 Cal.App.4th 816, 822 (1992) ("Section 473 allows the court to impose just conditions in setting aside a default. Such conditions might include the attorney fees and costs incurred by the plaintiff in obtaining the default judgment.")

Defendant Melovich is to respond to outstanding form interrogatories within 7 days of the date of order.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at jameshfleming@gmail.com.
